title: astragalus callistachys
intro: Astragalus callistachys, also known as milk vetch or Huang qi, is a plant species in the legume family native to China and Mongolia. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries, valued for its adaptogenic and immunomodulatory properties, which enable the body to resist stress and adapt to changing environments. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as the ability to boost the immune system and improve respiratory function. Astragalus callistachys is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, capsules, and tinctures, and is commonly combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
